metadata.content_note: Grade 6 English production SkillPackage with real guided mission and Skill Practice level banks.

subject: English
skill: Cite Textual Evidence and Make Inferences

lesson.story_mission: Enter Evidence Expedition, where readers solve Grade 6 English challenges with careful reading and explanation.
lesson.objective: I can cite textual evidence to support literal understanding and inferences in Grade 6 literary and informational texts.
lesson.concept_explanation: I can cite textual evidence to support literal understanding and inferences in Grade 6 literary and informational texts. Use the text, language, punctuation, and context to prove your thinking.
lesson.common_misconception: A reader may choose an unsupported answer, make an inference without evidence, quote inaccurately, or give a weak explanation.
lesson.real_world_connection: Middle school readers use this skill in novels, science articles, history sources, directions, debates, and assessments.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read carefully, name the clue or evidence, explain how it supports the answer, and check that your response matches the whole text.
lesson.mini_lesson: Step 1: read the text or sentence. Step 2: mark the clue. Step 3: choose or write the answer. Step 4: explain why the clue proves it.

story.text: Read This Page. Welcome to Evidence Expedition. In this Grade 6 English mission, you will use cite textual evidence and make inferences with real texts and questions. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: Read This Page. Learn the strategy, study the visual model, and use Listen when hearing a sentence, passage, vocabulary word, example, or evidence will help. Press Next after you can explain the steps.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: Read This Page. Watch the model. Notice how the reader slows down, checks meaning, and proves each answer with the text. Use Listen to hear the model read clearly.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: Read This Page. The demo walks through one example before you answer. Listen to the example, repeat useful language, and notice why the answer is supported.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: Read This Page. Practice uses a guided question. Press Read Question before answering, use the hint ladder if you need help, and use Listen for sentence, passage, vocabulary, or evidence reading.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: Read This Page. Challenge questions are a little closer and require careful reasoning. Press Read Question, listen when helpful, and choose or write the answer that best fits the text.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: Read This Page. The checkpoint checks your independent understanding. Press Read Question for each item, use relevant evidence or examples, and avoid unsupported guesses.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: Read This Page. You earned a badge for careful Grade 6 English work with cite textual evidence and make inferences. Press Next to see your profile and choose more practice.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: Read This Page. Your profile saves accuracy, hints, stars, and misconception notes. Choose Practice This Skill to open the Skill Practice Center levels, or return to the Grade 6 hub.
profile.label: Read This Page
